events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to evaluate a scenario about column/beam erection & plumb on a commercial project. The work stage is "rough-complete". The scenario says: "Progress photos for Column/beam erection & plumb show the current checkpoint scope finished for this area. The remaining visible work is cleanup, labeling, or follow-on finish work outside the checkpoint being scored." So the work is essentially done for this scope, but not fully closed out. There's no mention of defects or non-compliance. The reference materials are AISC 303 and OSHA 1926 Subpart R. We need to decide pass/fail/needs_more_info, risk, work_stage, component_conditions, percent_complete, value, sound_source, confidence, event_time, rate, order, workable, findings, actions, rationale, references.

Given the description, the work is rough-complete, no defects mentioned. So decision likely "pass" if no issues. Risk low. Work stage "rough-complete". Component conditions: none flagged, so empty list. Percent complete: maybe 100% for this scope? But it says "rough-complete" and remaining work is cleanup/labeling/finish outside checkpoint. So percent complete for the column/beam erection & plumb scope might be 100%? Or maybe 95%? I'll estimate 100% since the checkpoint scope is finished. Value: no numeric reading asked. Sound source, event_time, rate, order, workable: not relevant, so omit or set to null/empty. Findings: none, so empty list. Actions: none needed. Rationale: "Column/beam erection and plumb work is complete per progress photos; no defects observed; remaining work is cleanup and labeling outside scope." References: AISC 303, OSHA 1926 Subpart R (maybe just mention they were considered). Confidence: high, say 0.95.

I'll output JSON accordingly.
